---
title: "Unconfoundedness and Propensity Scores"
---

# LaTeX Equations from Chapter 2: Unconfoundedness and the Propensity Score

## Basic Unconfoundedness Assumption

Conditional independence (unconfoundedness):
$\{Y_i(0), Y_i(1)\} \perp W_i | X_i = x, \quad \text{for all } x \in \mathcal{X}$

More general form:
$\{Y_i(0), Y_i(1)\} \perp W_i | X_i$

## Conditional Average Treatment Effect

$\tau(x) = \mathbb{E}[Y_i(1) - Y_i(0) | X_i = x]$

## Stratified Estimation

Stratified estimator:
$\hat{\tau}_{STRAT} = \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} \frac{n_x}{n} \hat{\tau}(x)$

where:
$\hat{\tau}(x) = \frac{1}{n_{x1}} \sum_{\{X_i=x, W_i=1\}} Y_i - \frac{1}{n_{x0}} \sum_{\{X_i=x, W_i=0\}} Y_i$

Notation:
- $n_x = |\{i : X_i = x\}|$
- $n_{xw} = |\{i : X_i = x, W_i = w\}|$

## Theorem 2.1 (Stratified Estimation)

Central limit theorem for stratified estimator:
$\sqrt{n}(\hat{\tau}_{STRAT} - \tau) \Rightarrow N(0, V_{STRAT})$

Asymptotic variance:
$V_{STRAT} = \text{Var}[\tau(X_i)] + \mathbb{E}\left[\frac{\sigma^2_{(1)}(X_i)}{e(X_i)} + \frac{\sigma^2_{(0)}(X_i)}{1-e(X_i)}\right]$

where:
- $e(x) = \mathbb{P}[W_i = 1 | X_i = x]$ (propensity score)
- $\sigma^2_{(w)}(x) = \text{Var}[Y_i^{(w)} | X_i = x]$

## Propensity Score

Definition of propensity score:
$e(x) = \mathbb{P}[W_i = 1 | X_i = x]$

Balancing property:
$\{Y_i(0), Y_i(1)\} \perp W_i | e(X_i)$

Proof of balancing property:
$\begin{align}
\mathbb{P}[W_i = w | \{Y_i(w)\}, e(X_i)] &= \int_{\mathcal{X}} \mathbb{P}[W_i = w | \{Y_i(w)\}, X_i = x] \mathbb{P}[X_i = x | \{Y_i(w)\}, e(X_i)] dx \\
&= \int_{\mathcal{X}} \mathbb{P}[W_i = w | X_i = x] \mathbb{P}[X_i = x | \{Y_i(w)\}, e(X_i)] dx \quad \text{(unconf.)} \\
&= \begin{cases}
e(X_i) & \text{if } w = 1 \\
1 - e(X_i) & \text{else}
\end{cases}
\end{align}$

## Propensity Stratification

Sorting step:
$\hat{e}(X_{i_1}) \leq \hat{e}(X_{i_2}) \leq \ldots \leq \hat{e}(X_{i_n})$

Stratum-specific estimator:
$\hat{\tau}_j = \frac{\sum_{j=\lfloor(j-1)n/J\rfloor+1}^{\lfloor jn/J\rfloor} W_i Y_i}{\sum_{j=\lfloor(j-1)n/J\rfloor+1}^{\lfloor jn/J\rfloor} W_i} - \frac{\sum_{j=\lfloor(j-1)n/J\rfloor+1}^{\lfloor jn/J\rfloor} (1-W_i) Y_i}{\sum_{j=\lfloor(j-1)n/J\rfloor+1}^{\lfloor jn/J\rfloor} (1-W_i)}$

Propensity stratification estimator:
$\hat{\tau}_{PSTRAT} = \frac{1}{J} \sum_{j=1}^J \hat{\tau}_j$

## Inverse-Propensity Weighting (IPW)

IPW estimator with estimated propensity scores:
$\hat{\tau}_{IPW} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \left(\frac{W_i Y_i}{\hat{e}(X_i)} - \frac{(1-W_i) Y_i}{1-\hat{e}(X_i)}\right)$

Oracle IPW estimator (true propensity scores):
$\hat{\tau}^*_{IPW} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \left(\frac{W_i Y_i}{e(X_i)} - \frac{(1-W_i) Y_i}{1-e(X_i)}\right)$

## Theorem 2.2 (Oracle IPW)

Central limit theorem for oracle IPW:
$\sqrt{n}(\hat{\tau}^*_{IPW} - \tau) \Rightarrow N(0, V_{IPW^*})$

Asymptotic variance:
$V_{IPW^*} = \text{Var}[\tau(X_i)] + \mathbb{E}\left[\frac{(\mu_{(0)}(X_i) + (1-e(X_i))\tau(X_i))^2}{e(X_i)(1-e(X_i))}\right] + \mathbb{E}\left[\frac{\sigma^2_{(1)}(X_i)}{e(X_i)} + \frac{\sigma^2_{(0)}(X_i)}{1-e(X_i)}\right]$

Alternative decomposition in proof:
$\frac{W_i Y_i}{e(X_i)} - \frac{(1-W_i) Y_i}{1-e(X_i)} = \tau(X_i) + \left(\frac{W_i}{e(X_i)} - \frac{1-W_i}{1-e(X_i)}\right) c(X_i) + \frac{W_i \varepsilon_i^{(1)}}{e(X_i)} - \frac{(1-W_i) \varepsilon_i^{(0)}}{1-e(X_i)}$

where:
- $c(x) = \mu_{(0)}(x) + (1-e(x))\tau(x)$
- $\varepsilon_i^{(w)} = Y_i^{(w)} - \mu_{(w)}(X_i)$

## Variance Comparison

Relationship between IPW and stratified variances:
$V_{IPW^*} = V_{STRAT} + \mathbb{E}\left[\frac{(\mu_{(0)}(X_i) + (1-e(X_i))\tau(X_i))^2}{e(X_i)(1-e(X_i))}\right]$

Therefore: $V_{IPW^*} \geq V_{STRAT}$ (IPW is generally less efficient than stratification)

## Overlap Assumptions

Weak overlap:
$\mathbb{E}[1/(e(X_i)(1-e(X_i)))] < \infty$

Strong overlap:
$\eta \leq e(x) \leq 1-\eta \quad \text{for all } x \in \mathcal{X}$

for some $\eta > 0$.

## Connection Between Stratified and IPW

When $\mathcal{X}$ is discrete, the stratified estimator can be written as IPW:
$\hat{\tau}_{STRAT} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \left(\frac{W_i Y_i}{\hat{e}(X_i)} - \frac{(1-W_i) Y_i}{1-\hat{e}(X_i)}\right)$

where:
$\hat{e}(x) = \frac{n_{x1}}{n_x}$

## Auxiliary Functions and Notation

Conditional mean functions:
$\mu^{(w)}(x) = \mathbb{E}[Y_i^{(w)} | X_i = x]$

Conditional variance functions:
$\sigma^{(w)2}(x) = \text{Var}[Y_i^{(w)} | X_i = x]$

Sample sizes by stratum and treatment:
- $n_x = |\{i : X_i = x\}|$ (total in stratum $x$)
- $n_{x1} = |\{i : X_i = x, W_i = 1\}|$ (treated in stratum $x$)  
- $n_{x0} = |\{i : X_i = x, W_i = 0\}|$ (control in stratum $x$)

## Estimation Error Terms

Decomposition of stratified estimator:
$\hat{\tau}_{STRAT} = \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} \lambda(x)\tau(x) + \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} (\hat{\lambda}(x) - \lambda(x)) \tau(x) + \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} \lambda(x) (\hat{\tau}(x) - \tau(x)) + \sum_{x \in \mathcal{X}} (\hat{\lambda}(x) - \lambda(x)) (\hat{\tau}(x) - \tau(x))$

where:
- $\lambda(x) = \mathbb{P}[X_i = x]$ (true probability)
- $\hat{\lambda}(x) = n_x/n$ (empirical probability)

## Examples from Text

Palo Alto treatment effect calculation:
$\hat{\tau}_{PA} = \frac{5}{152 + 5} - \frac{122}{2362 + 122} \approx -1.7\%$

Geneva treatment effect calculation:
$\hat{\tau}_{GVA} = \frac{350}{350 + 581} - \frac{1979}{2278 + 1979} \approx -8.9\%$

Combined estimate:
$\hat{\tau} = \frac{2641}{2641 + 5188} \hat{\tau}_{PA} + \frac{5188}{2641 + 5188} \hat{\tau}_{GVA} \approx -6.5\%$
